content.full_text:
HILL & SANDFORD
A PARTNERSHIP OF PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
EL PRESIDIO BUILDING
800 PRESIDIO AVENUE
SANTA BARBARA, CALIFORNIA 93101
(805) 963-1453

September 23, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I have been authorized by the Board of Directors of the Goleta Water District to request advice from your office on the definition of "business position" as a conflict of interest disclosure item. The Goleta Water District is a county water district in Santa Barbara County. I am the District's General Counsel.

This request was generated at the time the Board adopted an amended conflict of interest code consistent with the recent amendments made by the F.P.P.C. including the required disclosure of "business positions." In my telephone conversation with your staff, I was referred to Government Code section 87103. In my view that code section is comprehensive. Therefore this request is to solicit any further clarification or expansion of the definition of business position than is found in 87103.

The background for this request is that the District was advised by independent counsel that a contract should not be awarded to a firm in which the spouse of one of the members of the Board of Directors had what was in that Board member's view, a remote interest in the business that had submitted the bid for that contract. The basis for that independent counsel's opinion was Government Code sections 1090 et seq. I understand that the criteria differ between that statutory scheme and the F.P.P.A. Nevertheless, that controversy generated this request for definition of business position.

Please contact me if you require any further information. I look forward to your response.

Very truly yours,

Russell R. Ruiz

RRR:tk
cc: Goleta Water District
